name: Saucey Black Beans
recipeIngredient: 4 cups dried black beans, soaked overnight
Grated peel from one orange (just the thin outer part)
1/2 can mild green chilis (or a whole can for chili lovers)
2 cans stewed or canned tomatoes (16 oz each)
4 cloves minced garlic, or an entire bulb of roasted garlic, peeled and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the soaking water from the beans and add the 12 cups of water.
2. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and cook at a fast simmer for 1 to 1.5 hours until the beans are just starting to soften.
3. Add all other ingredients except the molido and continue cooking until the beans are thoroughly soft.
4. Stir in the molido and turn off the burner. Allow to sit for 30 minutes.
5. Serve over rice or cornbread, or use in enchiladas.
6. Adjust cooking time according to taste; if the beans seem too liquidy after adding onions, leave the lid off until they cook down.
Note: Personal preference is to use 2 tablespoons of molido, but this may be excessive for some people. It would likely be good with fresh oregano if available.
